title: Quantification and Phenotyping of SARS-CoV-2-specific T Cell Response via Flow Cytometry
author: Mutavhatsindi, Riou
materials: BV421‚Ñ¢ mouse anti-human Granzyme B antibody
Alexa Fluor¬Æ 700 mouse anti-human IFN-Œ≥ antibody
Fresh blood collected in Sodium Heparin tubes
D-PBS liquid w/o Ca and Mg

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: This protocol provides reagents and specific steps for quantifying and immunophenotyping the¬ÝSARS-CoV-2 spike-specific T-cell response from whole blood samples. Here, we present an 11-color antibody panel designed to identify IFN-Œ≥, TNF-Œ±, and IL-2 production, memory differentiation markers (CD45RA and CD27), cytotoxic molecules (Granzyme-B and Perforin), and the inhibitory receptor, PD-1. Furthermore, blood stimulation can be performed with peptide pools from¬Ýother pathogens (examples are given in this protocol using a Mycobacterium tuberculosis (Mtb) specific-peptide pool). For the cell staining steps, other antibodies targeting different markers of interest can be included.

steps.title: Before you begin
steps.instruction: Before starting, it is essential to check the configuration of your instrument to ensure compatibility with the reagents used in the proposed panel.

steps.title: Cell staining for flow cytometry
steps.instruction: This section describes the steps to detect and phenotype SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific T¬Ýcells. Here, we used an 11-color panel to assess cytokine production (IFN-Œ≥, TNF-Œ±, and IL-2), memory differentiation (CD45RA and CD27), cytotoxic potential (Granzyme-B and Perforin), and the expression of the inhibitory receptor, PD-1.28.Prepare the fluorophore-conjugated antibody mix in a 1.5¬ÝmL Eppendorf tube according to Table¬Ý2. Start by adding the 1√ó eBioscience Permeabilization solution (see materials and equipment section) then the BD Horizon Brilliant Stain Buffer plus and then each antibody.Table¬Ý2Fluorophore-conjugated antibody mixTargetFluorophoreVolume per stainVolume for 20 stains (+10% overage)IFN-Œ≥Alexa 7000.2¬ÝŒºL4.4¬ÝŒºLGranzyme-BBV4210.3¬ÝŒºL6.6¬ÝŒºLTNF-Œ±FITC0.4¬ÝŒºL8.8¬ÝŒºLPD1PE0.5¬ÝŒºL11.0¬ÝŒºLCD45RABV6050.5¬ÝŒºL11.0¬ÝŒºLCD4PE-Cy70.5¬ÝŒºL11.0¬ÝŒºLCD3BV7850.6¬ÝŒºL13.2¬ÝŒºLIL-2PE/Dazzle 5940.7¬ÝŒºL15.4¬ÝŒºLPerforinAPC0.9¬ÝŒºL19.8¬ÝŒºLCD27PE-Cy51.0¬ÝŒºL22.0¬ÝŒºLCD8BV5101.3¬ÝŒºL28.6¬ÝŒºLBD Brilliant Stain Buffer Plus10.0¬ÝŒºL220¬ÝŒºL1√ó eBioscience Permeabilization solution (q.s 50¬ÝŒºL)33.1¬ÝŒºL728.2¬ÝŒºL29.Vortex the antibody mix.30.Centrifuge the antibody mix at 12,000¬Ýrpm (‚àº8,500 rcf) for 4¬Ýmin in a mini centrifuge.31.Store at 4¬∞C in the dark until use.CRITICAL: BD Brilliant Stain Buffer Plus reduces possible compensation artifacts when using multiple Brilliant Violet (BV) reagents in the same flow panel. This buffer is formulated to allow for reduced test volume (10¬ÝŒºL/test), avoiding excessive dilution of the permeabilization buffer during staining.CRITICAL: Keep all antibodies and buffers on ice while preparing the antibody mix.CRITICAL: The centrifugation of the antibody staining mix (step 31) will pellet antibody aggregates that could lead to non-specific staining.CRITICAL: The panel‚Äôs performance depends on the sensitivity and setting of the cytometer and reagents. Failing to titrate reagents properly may result in off-scale events or a lack of optimal separation between the negative and positive populations. It is thus critical to properly calibrate the instrument and titrate reagents before the experiment (Maciorowski et¬Ýal., 2017).Note: To aid with the discrimination of positive events, one can set up fluorescent minus one (FMO) controls, where the marker of interest is omitted from the staining mix (Roederer, 2002). In this protocol, we strongly recommend using FMO controls for the PD-1 antibody.Note: Additionally, a ‚Äúdump‚Äù channel can be added (for example CD14 and CD19) to exclude specific cell subsets from the analyses.32.Add 50¬ÝŒºL of antibody mix to each well.33.Mix well by pipetting up and down (8‚Äì10 times), while avoiding creating excessive bubbles.34.Incubate for 45¬Ýmin at room temperature in the dark.35.Add 170¬ÝŒºL of 1√ó eBioscience Permeabilization solution.36.Centrifuge at 500¬Ý√ó¬Ýg for 4¬Ýmin at room temperature.37.Discard supernatant by flicking the plate (rapidly invert the plate over a waste container and gently tap the plate on a paper towel to remove excess fluid).38.Add 200¬ÝŒºL of 1√ó eBioscience Permeabilization solution and mix well by pipetting up and down (8‚Äì10 times).39.Centrifuge at 500¬Ý√ó¬Ýg for 4¬Ýmin at room temperature.40.Discard supernatant by flicking the plate.41.Add 100¬ÝŒºL of 1% formaldehyde solution and mix well by pipetting up and down (8‚Äì10 times).42.Incubate for 15¬Ýmin at room temperature in the dark.43.Add 100¬ÝŒºL of PBS.44.Centrifuge at 500¬Ý√ó¬Ýg for 4¬Ýmin at room temperature.45.Resuspend in 160¬ÝŒºL of PBS and transfer to prelabeled Microtiter tubes and store cells at 4¬∞C protected from light until ready to acquire on the cytometer.Figure¬Ý3The absence of viability dye in the presented protocol does not significantly affect the assay‚Äôs performance(A) Example of viability dye staining of lymphocytes (CD3 positive and CD3 negative cells).(B) Frequencies of dead cells in CD3 positive and CD3 negative lymphocytes (n=30). Bars represent medians.(C) Frequencies of Mtb-specific CD4 T¬Ýcells producing IFN-Œ≥, TNF-Œ±, or IL-2 with or without exclusion of dead cells¬Ý(n=30). P values are reported on top of the graph. Satistical comparisons were performed using the Wilcoxon matched-pairs signed rank test.Note: To ensure the procedure is simple and rapid with minimal perturbation of the samples (i.e., no blood dilution, no erythrocyte lysis prior to stimulation), the usage of a viability dye to differentiate live and dead cells was not included in this protocol. To ensure that this approach does not skew the detection of antigen-specific T-cells, whole blood assays set up to measure Mtb-specific CD4+ T-cell responses were performed in the presence of a viability dye (Riou et¬Ýal., 2020). Briefly, whole blood was stimulated for 5¬Ýh with Mtb peptide, red blood cells were lysed, and live leucocytes were then stained with a viability dye. Cells were then fixed and cryopreserved until batch staining. Data were analyzed to define: 1) the extent of cell death in our assay, and 2) whether the absence of a viability dye affects the quantification of the antigen-specific T-cell response (Figure¬Ý3). Our data show that cell death in the T-cell compartment was minimal (median: 0.19%), and the detection of cytokine-producing CD4 T-cells was comparable with or without the exclusion of dead cells. Thus, because blood is processed fresh and the stimulation time before cell fixation is short (5 h), the absence of a viability dye in the staining process does not significantly impact the quality of the results.

steps.title: Preparation of flow cytometry compensation controls
Preparation of flow cytometry compensation controls
steps.instruction: This section describes how to prepare single stain controls using commercial beads to calculate compensation and correct for spectral overlap between fluorophores.46.Prepare 14 Polystyrene tubes (5¬ÝmL) labeled as describe in Figure¬Ý4.Figure¬Ý4Experimental setup of compensation tubes47.Add 100¬ÝŒºL of 1√ó eBioscience Permeabilization solution at the bottom of each compensation tube.48.Add 15¬ÝŒºL of anti-Rat or anti-Mouse Ig compensation beads at the bottom of the appropriate tubes.49.Add 1¬ÝŒºL of antibodies to the designated tube.50.Vortex tubes gently.51.Incubate for 10¬Ýmin at room temperature in the dark.52.Add 2¬ÝmL of PBS 2% FBS, vortex the tubes.53.Centrifuge at 500¬Ýg for 10¬Ýmin at room temperature.54.Discard supernatant.55.Resuspend in 200¬ÝŒºL 1% formaldehyde solution.56.Incubate for 15¬Ýmin.57.Add 2¬ÝmL of PBS, vortex the tubes.58.Centrifuge at 500¬Ýg for 10¬Ýmin at room temperature.59.Discard supernatant.60.Resuspend in 200¬ÝŒºL of PBS 2% FBS and keep at 4¬∞C protected from light until use.CRITICAL: Since compensation beads precipitate rapidly, vortex the beads thoroughly and regularly before adding them to the 5¬ÝmL FACS tubes.CRITICAL: Since compensation control beads are coated with a species-specific antibody, it is important to choose beads that will bind the host species of the fluorochrome-conjugated antibody used. Based on the antibody panel used in this protocol, only one antibody (IL-2 PE dazzle‚Ñ¢ 594) is produced in rat, with the remainder being produced in mouse.Pause point: We have not extensively tested saving the samples in the fridge overnight after staining. However, in the case that samples cannot be immediately acquired by the flow cytometer,¬Ýthe cells can be saved for 16‚Äì20¬Ýh at 4¬∞C, protected from light.

steps.title: Quantification and statistical analysis
steps.instruction: We used FlowJo v10.7.1 to analyze flow cytometry data. SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific T¬Ýcells were presented as the percentage of CD8+ or CD4+ T¬Ýcells that express IFN-Œ≥, TNF-Œ± or IL-2. The gating strategy is shown in Figure¬Ý5. A positive response was defined as any cytokine response that was at least twice the background of unstimulated cells and data are reported after background subtraction. To define the phenotype of SARS-CoV-2-specific T¬Ýcells, a cut-off of 30 events was used to ensure the accuracy of the measurements (Figure¬Ý7). Data visualization and statistical analyses were performed using GraphPad Prism v9.1.0.Figure¬Ý7Assessment of the minimal number of cells required to define the phenotype antigen-specific T¬Ýcells(A) By adjusting the time gate, we performed repeated (non-overlapping) measurements of CD27 expression on antigen-specific T¬Ýcells (ranging from 10 to 100 cells) to define the variability of the measurement.(B) Summary data showing the expression of CD27 on antigen-specific CD4 T¬Ýcells from repeated measurements of a defined number of antigen-specific CD4 T¬Ýcells (10‚Äì100). The number of measures done for each sub-group is indicated at the bottom of the graph (n¬Ý= 83 to n¬Ý= 9). The coefficients of variation are indicated at the top of the graph. Bars represent the mean with standard deviation.
